refined_fr.refined_fr: Enable measurement and reporting of material throughput in volumetric or mass units per unit time.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates two distinct obligations: (1) the system's capability to measure/report throughput metrics, and (2) the achievement of a customer-defined target. The latter is a success criterion dependent on operational execution, not a system requirement. The refined FR isolates the system's functional obligation—providing visibility into throughput—which is solution-neutral and testable independent of whether a specific productivity target is met. The target itself belongs in acceptance criteria or KPI definitions, not as a functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that the NGHE solution captures and reports material throughput (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations. Verify data accuracy against independent measurement or calibration standards. Confirm that reported metrics are accessible to the Site Manager and Equipment Operator in real time or near-real time.
sub_frs: Acquire material throughput data (volume or mass per time interval) from operational sensors or load cells.
Calculate and aggregate throughput metrics in standardized units (m³/h or ton/h).
Display or export throughput metrics to authorized stakeholders (Site Manager, Equipment Operator) with timestamp and operational context.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire instantaneous flow rate via continuous sensing of material stream properties (mass or volume per unit time) and integrate over evaluation period to compute cumulative throughput
DP2: Periodically sample material quantity at fixed time intervals, compute differential changes, and extrapolate to period-level throughput estimate
DP3: Instrument material input and output boundaries with independent accumulation counters (mass or volume), compute net throughput as difference, and normalize by elapsed time
DP4: Establish reference material batches of known quantity, measure batch processing cycle time, and derive throughput as batch quantity divided by cycle duration
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP3: Instrument material input and output boundaries with independent accumulation counters (mass or volume), compute net throughput as difference, and normalize by elapsed time

potential_sources: Input and output counter calibration drift (mitigated by independent sensing paths)
Time synchronization between input/output measurements (low risk if counters are polled synchronously)
Assumption that input and output boundaries are physically distinct and accessible (worksite layout dependency)
coupling_check.notes: DP3 exhibits low functional coupling: input measurement is logically independent of output measurement, and both feed a simple arithmetic operation (difference and normalization). This decoupling supports fault isolation and independent validation. DP1 risks tight coupling between sensing modality and integration algorithm; DP2 introduces extrapolation bias; DP4 couples throughput inference to batch size variability.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Material input and output streams are physically separable and measurable at distinct boundaries. Risk: If material is recycled or diverted mid-process, net throughput may not reflect actual processing capacity.

Assumption: Accumulation counters (mass or volume) can be read with sufficient precision and frequency to meet productivity_target acceptance criterion. Risk: If counter resolution is coarse relative to target throughput, reported values may fall below target due to quantization error rather than operational failure.
Assumption: Evaluation period is long enough to amortize sensor drift and transient startup/shutdown effects. Risk: Short evaluation windows may yield non-representative throughput estimates.

Assumption: Worksite safety plan permits instrumentation of input/output boundaries without disrupting material flow or creating hazards. Risk: If instrumentation is intrusive, operational policies may restrict deployment.

Assumption: Time reference (elapsed time denominator) is stable and synchronized across measurement system. Risk: Clock drift or asynchronous polling could introduce systematic bias in throughput calculation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR to explicitly state: (1) acceptable measurement uncertainty or tolerance band around productivity_target, (2) minimum evaluation period duration, (3) whether recycled or diverted material must be accounted for separately, and (4) physical accessibility constraints at input/output boundaries. These clarifications will reduce ambiguity in DP selection and IM realization (e.g., choice of counter technology, polling frequency, and boundary instrumentation strategy).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized material flow monitoring and throughput computation subsystem that instruments input/output boundaries, maintains independent accumulation counters, and derives net throughput metrics normalized by elapsed time.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmit computed net throughput, accumulation counter states, and material flow metrics to external monitoring/logging systems

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive configuration parameters for counter initialization, boundary definitions, and throughput computation intervals

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Signal anomalous throughput or counter drift conditions that may indicate system malfunction or safety concern

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent containment: material flow instrumentation, accumulation logic, and throughput computation are core NGHE functions. Counter state management and time-normalized calculations are naturally localized. Minor diffusion: telemetry export and safety alerting require external interfaces, but these are well-defined and minimal.

Poor containment: Vehicle is a physical platform; material flow monitoring is a logical/computational function orthogonal to vehicle dynamics, propulsion, or navigation. High diffusion: counter logic, accumulation state, and throughput computation would scatter across vehicle subsystems without clear ownership.

Poor containment: Teleoperation is a command/control interface layer, not a material processing or monitoring subsystem. Accumulation counters and throughput computation are not teleoperation concerns. High diffusion: would require teleoperation to own stateful material flow logic, violating separation of concerns.

Weak containment: Safety Services may consume throughput anomalies for alerting, but should not own the instrumentation, accumulation, or computation logic itself. That is a material-handling concern, not a safety concern. Diffusion: mixing material flow measurement with safety response logic creates tight coupling and unclear ownership.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The DP (material input/output instrumentation with independent accumulation counters and net throughput normalization) is a core material-handling measurement and analytics function. It belongs in the NGHE Solution module because: (1) NGHE is responsible for material processing and flow control; (2) accumulation counters and throughput metrics are intrinsic to NGHE state and performance monitoring; (3) the computation is deterministic and stateful, requiring a single authoritative owner; (4) external consumers (Telemetry, SafetyAlert) receive derived metrics, not raw instrumentation logic.
containment_risks: If Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems independently instrument their own material boundaries, counter state will fragment and net throughput computation will become inconsistent. Mitigation: enforce single-source-of-truth ownership in NGHE Solution.

If Safety Services owns the accumulation logic to detect anomalies, the boundary between material measurement and safety response becomes blurred. Mitigation: Safety Services consumes throughput metrics from NGHE Solution via SafetyAlert interface.

If elapsed time synchronization is not tightly managed (e.g., clock drift between counter updates and time normalization), throughput accuracy degrades. Mitigation: ensure NGHE Solution uses a single, authoritative time source.

If external systems (e.g., Telemetry consumers) attempt to recompute throughput from raw counter exports, they may diverge from NGHE's canonical value. Mitigation: export only normalized throughput metrics, not raw counters, via Telemetry interface.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The FR (enable measurement and reporting of material throughput in volumetric or mass units per unit time) is directly verifiable against acceptance criteria: reported productivity ≥ productivity_target over evaluation period. NGHE Solution IM boundary provides a single authoritative source for throughput computation, enabling deterministic measurement and audit trail. Telemetry interface exports normalized metrics suitable for external validation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. NGHE Solution is the unambiguous owner of material flow instrumentation, accumulation counter state, and throughput normalization logic. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) establish well-defined boundaries for configuration input, metric export, and anomaly signaling. No ambiguity regarding which module is responsible for FR realization.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP3 (independent accumulation counters at input/output boundaries, net throughput as difference, time normalization) is a sound and complete solution to the FR. The logic is deterministic, measurable, and directly traceable to the functional requirement. Counter-based accumulation is a standard, proven approach for material flow measurement. Time normalization ensures throughput is expressed in the required units (per unit time). No logical gaps or ambiguities detected.

diffusion_risks: Fragmentation risk: If Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems independently instrument material boundaries or maintain shadow counters, net throughput computation will diverge from NGHE's canonical value. Mitigation: enforce single-source-of-truth ownership; all material boundary instrumentation must route through NGHE Solution.

Clock synchronization risk: If elapsed time for throughput normalization is sourced from multiple time references (e.g., vehicle clock vs. NGHE local clock), throughput accuracy degrades. Mitigation: NGHE Solution must use a single, authoritative time source; document time source selection and synchronization strategy.

Counter state leakage risk: If raw accumulation counters are exported via Telemetry without normalization, external consumers may attempt independent throughput recomputation, introducing divergence. Mitigation: Telemetry interface should export only normalized throughput metrics and derived analytics, not raw counter state.

Safety response coupling risk: If Safety Services owns or duplicates accumulation logic to detect throughput anomalies, ownership becomes ambiguous and maintenance burden increases. Mitigation: Safety Services consumes SafetyAlert signals from NGHE Solution; NGHE Solution is sole owner of measurement and anomaly detection logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment (0.92 score) in NGHE Solution. Material flow instrumentation, accumulation counter logic, and throughput computation are core NGHE responsibilities and naturally localized within the module boundary. Minor diffusion (0.08) is appropriate: telemetry export and safety alerting are external interfaces, not internal diffusion. All other candidate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) show poor containment (0.28–0.42) and high diffusion (0.58–0.72), confirming NGHE Solution is the correct owner. No architectural packaging issues detected; DP and IM are well-aligned.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document the time source and synchronization mechanism for elapsed time normalization in NGHE Solution design specification. Specify tolerance for clock drift and mitigation strategy (e.g., periodic resynchronization, monotonic clock guarantees).

Revise Telemetry interface specification to clarify that only normalized throughput metrics (e.g., kg/min, m³/hr) and derived analytics are exported; raw accumulation counter state should not be transmitted to avoid external recomputation divergence.

Add a design constraint to the NGHE Solution module: 'All material boundary instrumentation and accumulation counter logic shall be owned and executed exclusively by NGHE Solution. No other subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) shall maintain independent counters or recompute throughput.'

Define the SafetyAlert interface contract: specify which throughput anomalies trigger alerts (e.g., counter drift exceeding threshold, net throughput deviation from expected range), and ensure Safety Services consumes only these derived signals, not raw counter state.

Add acceptance test: verify that throughput reported by NGHE Solution matches independent measurement (e.g., manual calibration, external flow meter) within ±5% over representative evaluation period, confirming measurement accuracy and time normalization correctness.

decision.justification: FR4 (Enable measurement and reporting of material throughput in volumetric or mass units per unit time) has passed all three decomposition gates with high confidence. The FR is functionally complete, logically sound (DP3: independent accumulation counters at input/output boundaries with time normalization), and architecturally well-contained within NGHE Solution (0.92 containment score, minimal diffusion). The FR is directly verifiable against acceptance criteria (reported productivity ≥ productivity_target). Further decomposition into sub-FRs is not warranted: throughput measurement is a leaf-level functional capability that does not decompose into independent sub-functions without introducing artificial fragmentation. The recommended revisions address implementation detail (time source documentation, Telemetry interface clarification, design constraints, SafetyAlert contract, acceptance testing) and should be captured in the design specification phase, not as sub-FR decomposition. High safety criticality reinforces the need for rigorous specification and testing, but does not necessitate deeper functional decomposition.

revise.why: DP3 is sound and complete, but the design specification must be enriched with implementation details to mitigate identified diffusion risks and support safety-critical verification. Specifically: (1) explicitly document the time source and synchronization mechanism for elapsed time normalization, including tolerance for clock drift and resynchronization strategy; (2) clarify that Telemetry interface exports only normalized throughput metrics and derived analytics, not raw accumulation counter state; (3) define the SafetyAlert interface contract, specifying which throughput anomalies trigger alerts and ensuring Safety Services consumes only derived signals; (4) add a design constraint that all material boundary instrumentation and accumulation counter logic are exclusively owned by NGHE Solution. These revisions strengthen DP3 without changing its fundamental logic and prepare the design for detailed implementation and safety validation.

assumptions: NGHE Solution has exclusive ownership of material flow instrumentation and accumulation counter state; no other subsystem maintains independent counters.
A single, authoritative time source is available and synchronized across NGHE Solution for elapsed time normalization; clock drift is bounded and managed.
Telemetry interface exports only normalized throughput metrics (e.g., kg/min, m³/hr) and derived analytics; raw accumulation counter state is not transmitted externally.
Safety Services consumes SafetyAlert signals derived from NGHE Solution's throughput computation; Safety Services does not independently recompute throughput or maintain shadow counters.

Material boundary instrumentation (sensors, flow meters, mass scales) is physically integrated with or directly connected to NGHE Solution; no intermediate subsystems introduce measurement latency or divergence.
Throughput measurement accuracy is achievable within ±5% over the evaluation period, as verified by independent calibration or external flow meter comparison.
open_risks: Clock synchronization risk: If elapsed time for throughput normalization is sourced from multiple time references (vehicle clock vs. NGHE local clock), throughput accuracy degrades. Mitigation: document time source selection, synchronization strategy, and clock drift tolerance in design specification.

Counter state leakage risk: If raw accumulation counters are exported via Telemetry without normalization, external consumers may attempt independent throughput recomputation, introducing divergence. Mitigation: enforce Telemetry interface specification to export only normalized metrics.

Fragmentation risk: If Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems independently instrument material boundaries or maintain shadow counters, net throughput computation will diverge from NGHE's canonical value. Mitigation: add design constraint enforcing single-source-of-truth ownership; all material boundary instrumentation must route through NGHE Solution.

Safety response coupling risk: If Safety Services owns or duplicates accumulation logic to detect throughput anomalies, ownership becomes ambiguous and maintenance burden increases. Mitigation: define SafetyAlert interface contract; Safety Services consumes only derived signals, not raw counter state.

Sensor failure or drift: Material boundary instrumentation (flow meters, mass scales) may fail or drift over time, introducing systematic measurement error. Mitigation: implement sensor health monitoring, periodic calibration, and anomaly detection in NGHE Solution; export sensor status via Telemetry.

High safety criticality: Throughput measurement is used for operational decision-making and safety-critical functions (e.g., material containment, hazard detection). Measurement errors could propagate to safety-critical logic. Mitigation: rigorous acceptance testing (±5% accuracy), design review, and traceability to safety requirements.
evidence_needed: Design specification for NGHE Solution detailing time source selection, synchronization mechanism, clock drift tolerance, and resynchronization strategy.
Revised Telemetry interface specification clarifying that only normalized throughput metrics and derived analytics are exported; raw accumulation counter state is not transmitted.
Design constraint document: 'All material boundary instrumentation and accumulation counter logic shall be owned and executed exclusively by NGHE Solution.'

SafetyAlert interface contract specifying which throughput anomalies trigger alerts (e.g., counter drift exceeding threshold, net throughput deviation from expected range) and ensuring Safety Services consumes only derived signals.

Acceptance test plan and results: verify that throughput reported by NGHE Solution matches independent measurement (e.g., manual calibration, external flow meter) within ±5% over representative evaluation period.
Traceability matrix linking FR4 to safety requirements, design constraints, and acceptance criteria.
Sensor health monitoring and anomaly detection design for material boundary instrumentation; periodic calibration procedure and schedule.
